source_jobs.title: General Interest
source_jobs.description:
Fortreum is a trusted leader in cloud and cybersecurity services, ranked among the Top 5 FedRAMP Third Party Assessment Organizations (3PAO). With the addition of Kovr.AI, we have expanded our capabilities to include advanced cyber risk quantification and analytics, enabling organizations to better understand, measure, and communicate risk. Together, we deliver independent, third-party, vendor-agnostic regulatory assessment and advisory services, alongside advanced cybersecurity offensive and compliance technical solutions. Our comprehensive service portfolio spans regulatory compliance frameworks including FedRAMP, CMMC, FISMA, SOC, PCI, ISO, and HIPAA.

Working with Fortune 500 companies and leading cloud service providers, we’ve built our reputation on service-delivery excellence and an unwavering commitment to client success. Our continued rapid growth creates exceptional opportunities for driven professionals to make their mark in the cybersecurity industry—guided by our core values: quality above all, a customer-driven mindset, autonomy, and personal accountability.





This is a general opportunity bucket and does not align with an open opportunity. 


What Fortreum Offers

We offer a competitive compensation package, where you will be rewarded based on your performance/outcomes and recognized for the value you bring to our business. Our benefits package includes medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, 401K plan with a 4% match, company paid short-term disability, company paid long-term disability, company paid AD&D and life insurance, flex time off, annual bonuses, training stipends, certification reimbursements, access to over 30,000 free online training courses, personal cell phone allowance, new hire and annual home office stipend, spot awards and eleven paid holidays.



An Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer

Fortreum is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. If you’d like to view a copy of the company’s affirmative action plan or policy statement, please email hr@fortreum.com. If you have a disability and you believe you need a reasonable accommodation in order to search for a job opening or to submit an online application, please e-mail hr@fortreum.com or call 703-594-1460. This email and phone number is created exclusively to assist disabled job seekers whose disability prevents them from being able to apply online. Only messages left for this purpose will be returned. Messages left for other purposes, such as following up on an application or technical issues not related to a disability, will not receive a response.

In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form upon hire.

source_jobs.title: Federal Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Fortreum is a trusted leader in cloud and cybersecurity services, ranked among the Top 5 FedRAMP Third Party Assessment Organizations (3PAO). With the addition of Kovr.AI, we have expanded our capabilities to include advanced cyber risk quantification and analytics, enabling organizations to better understand, measure, and communicate risk. Together, we deliver independent, third-party, vendor-agnostic regulatory assessment and advisory services, alongside advanced cybersecurity offensive and compliance technical solutions. Our comprehensive service portfolio spans regulatory compliance frameworks including FedRAMP, CMMC, FISMA, SOC, PCI, ISO, and HIPAA.

Working with Fortune 500 companies and leading cloud service providers, we’ve built our reputation on service-delivery excellence and an unwavering commitment to client success. Our continued rapid growth creates exceptional opportunities for driven professionals to make their mark in the cybersecurity industry—guided by our core values: quality above all, a customer-driven mindset, autonomy, and personal accountability.




THE OPPORTUNITY

ATO acceleration and risk observability are the top priorities for every government agency leader. Kovr.AI, Fortreum's AI-native and patented platform, is the only solution designed to deliver this at scale. We are looking for a high-performing Federal Account Executive to capitalize on Fortreum's tremendous momentum and pipeline velocity. In this role, you'll get to work with the world's best cyber technologists to address the most pressing needs of government executives, managing a $3M quota and executing complex, high-value enterprise sales. This is a role for a "hunter" who understands how to navigate the intersection of direct sales and a robust partner ecosystem.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Drive Growth: Execute a strategic territory plan for Fortreum, focusing on enterprise-level security software and services.

 - Strategic Engagement: Build and maintain relationships with CIOs, CISOs, and SES/GS-15 level leaders across civilian and defense agencies to showcase the value of the Kovr.AI platform.

 - Ecosystem Orchestration: Work closely with our premier partner network, including Blackwood Associates, AWS, Accenture Federal Services, and Carahsoft, to co-sell and expand Fortreum's market reach.

 - Pipeline Velocity: Manage a high-velocity sales cycle from discovery to close, leveraging Fortreum's existing momentum and pre-authorized status (FedRAMP/IL5) to shorten procurement timelines.

 - Mission Alignment: Translate complex technical capabilities of the Kovr.AI platform into mission-driven outcomes for government stakeholders.
   
   


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - Demonstrated track record of quota attainment in the Federal sector, specifically including at least one $2M+ enterprise contract.

 - Deep experience selling security software (SaaS) and tech-enabled services.

 - A bias for action with a proven "hunter" mindset and a repeatable methodology for breaking into new agencies.

 - Expert at navigating both direct sales motions and channel-led strategies through major federal systems integrators and VARs.

 - High-level communication skills capable of commanding a room of senior government executives and technical leaders.

 - Active Top Secret clearance is required.
   
   


POSTED SALARY RANGE

$140,000-150,000 annual salary, with $290,000-300,000 OTE


What Fortreum Offers

We offer a competitive compensation package, where you will be rewarded based on your performance/outcomes and recognized for the value you bring to our business. Our benefits package includes medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, 401K plan with a 4% match, company paid short-term disability, company paid long-term disability, company paid AD&D and life insurance, flex time off, annual bonuses, training stipends, certification reimbursements, access to over 30,000 free online training courses, personal cell phone allowance, new hire

source_jobs.title: Cleared Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Fortreum is a trusted leader in cloud and cybersecurity services, ranked among the Top 5 FedRAMP Third Party Assessment Organizations (3PAO). With the addition of Kovr.AI, we have expanded our capabilities to include advanced cyber risk quantification and analytics, enabling organizations to better understand, measure, and communicate risk. Together, we deliver independent, third-party, vendor-agnostic regulatory assessment and advisory services, alongside advanced cybersecurity offensive and compliance technical solutions. Our comprehensive service portfolio spans regulatory compliance frameworks including FedRAMP, CMMC, FISMA, SOC, PCI, ISO, and HIPAA.

Working with Fortune 500 companies and leading cloud service providers, we’ve built our reputation on service-delivery excellence and an unwavering commitment to client success. Our continued rapid growth creates exceptional opportunities for driven professionals to make their mark in the cybersecurity industry—guided by our core values: quality above all, a customer-driven mindset, autonomy, and personal accountability.




THE OPPORTUNITY

On our team, you will have the opportunity to work with industry-leading experts who’ve supported the world’s most security-conscious organizations. As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you will embed with enterprise and federal customers to deploy, integrate, and operationalize the Kovr AI Platform within their environments—building integrations with their DevSecOps toolchains, automating evidence collection and continuous monitoring, and configuring compliance workflows that accelerate Authority to Operate (ATO). You will serve as the hands-on technical bridge between our customers and Kovr’s engineering team, solving complex deployment challenges across cloud, hybrid, and classified National Security and Intelligence Community (IC) environments.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deploy, configure, and operationalize the Kovr AI Platform within customer environments, including cloud, hybrid, and on-premises deployments supporting IL2-IL6 requirements

 - Build and maintain integrations between the platform and customers’ existing DevSecOps toolchains, CI/CD pipelines, and compliance workflows

 - Develop automation for evidence collection, continuous monitoring, and RMF authorization processes, including SSP generation, control mapping, and POA&M workflows

 - Write and maintain code, scripts, and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Terraform, Helm) to provision and configure platform components and customer integrations

 - Customize and extend platform workflows and data pipelines to meet each customer’s compliance framework requirements (NIST 800-53, FedRAMP, CMMC, RMF)

 - Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues across deployment, integration, data ingestion, and performance, often on-site or within classified environments

 - Serve as the hands-on technical bridge between customers and Kovr’s engineering team, translating real-world deployment needs into product requirements and feature requests

 - Partner with the DevOps team to ensure secure, reliable, and compliant operation of customer deployments, including monitoring, logging, and incident response

 - Lead technical onboarding and enablement for customer engineering teams to drive successful adoption and self-sufficiency

 - Produce technical documentation, runbooks, and deployment guides that support repeatable, scalable implementations




BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active Top Secret/SCI security clearance with Full Scope Polygraph and active Cleared Contractor Access (CCA).

 - 5+ years of software engineering, systems engineering, or DevOps experience in enterprise or federal IT environments

 - Hands-on experience deploying and integrating software across cloud platforms (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government, GCP) and hybrid or on-premises environments

 - Strong proficiency with DevSecOps pipelines, CI/CD workflows, and containerized deployments (Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, ArgoCD

notes: Cybersecurity services company with a mix of technical and non-technical roles
